source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: AI-generated social science data remain highly controversial
Before AI-generated data can be used for social inquiry, it is essential to rigorously evaluate their validity
The validity of AI-generated synthetic data should be evaluated in terms of their population-level statistical realism
We present a systematic benchmark, SSDataBench, designed to assess whether LLM-generated data can reproduce key statistical patterns
Our results reveal several limitations of current LLMs as they tend to generate data that accentuate typological patterns
We outline several promising pathways toward improved statistical realism in future work
We hope that future developments in LLM-based social simulation will explicitly integrate the measurement and preservation of population-level statistical patterns

typical_pattern: State the controversy or problem in the field
Establish the need for rigorous evaluation
Present the proposed framework or approach
Acknowledge limitations of the current work
Point to future directions and implications

likely_priorities: Justification of subjective methodological choices in benchmark design
Validity of using survey data as ground truth
Transparency about which LLMs may have been trained on survey data
likely_attacks: Pass rates involve necessarily subjective decisions and should be interpreted comparatively rather than absolutely
Traditional imputation methods are not directly applicable under sparse-conditioning settings
Survey data do not constitute perfect ground truth given common problems with survey research
Unknown whether evaluated LLMs have already benefited from existing survey data
Limited evaluation under sparse-conditioning setting may not reflect performance in other scenarios
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly acknowledges several limitations: subjective decisions in pass rate calculation (bootstrap sample size, summary statistics, statistical tests, significance threshold), evaluation primarily under sparse-conditioning settings where traditional imputation methods are not directly applicable, use of surveys as imperfect ground truth given various biases, and uncertainty about whether evaluated LLMs were already trained on survey data. These self-identified limitations suggest the authors anticipate reviewer concerns about methodological choices, comparability with existing methods, validity of the evaluation baseline, and potential confounding from pre-training.

examples.quoted_phrase: Our results reveal several limitations of current LLMs under the evaluated setting
LLMs struggle to reproduce real-world distributions, often collapsing toward overly typical values
the models often draw on stereotyped priors, leading to associations that are frequently exaggerated
our preliminary experiments suggest that fine-tuning LLMs on survey data substantially improves statistical realism
If we can generate data with AI, it may be possible to generate outcomes associated with different counterfactual treatment conditions

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legends.
